seo_description: Current time in Tulsa, Oklahoma. Central Time Zone — CST (UTC-6) in winter, CDT (UTC-5) during DST. Live clock and time differences for Tulsa, OK.

faq.question: What time is it in Tulsa right now?
faq.answer: Tulsa, Oklahoma, USA uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Tulsa is on the same time as Oklahoma City, Dallas, and Chicago — 1 hour behind New York and 1 hour ahead of Denver. The live clock above shows the current Tulsa time.

faq.question: What time zone is Tulsa in?
faq.answer: Tulsa, Oklahoma is in the Central Time Zone (CT). It uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Tulsa shares its time zone with Oklahoma City, Dallas, Houston, and Kansas City.

faq.question: Does Tulsa observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Tulsa, Oklahoma observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (CST → CDT) and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Oklahoma follows the standard US DST schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tulsa and New York?
faq.answer: Tulsa (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 1 hour behind New York (EST/EDT, Eastern Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 8:00 AM in Tulsa.

faq.question: Is Tulsa in the same time zone as Oklahoma City?
faq.answer: Yes. Tulsa and Oklahoma City are both in the Central Time Zone (IANA: America/Chicago). They are approximately 100 miles apart on I-44 and always share the same time — CST (UTC-6) in winter and CDT (UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Tulsa?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Tulsa are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT, Monday through Friday. Tulsa is Oklahoma's second-largest city, historically known as the 'Oil Capital of the World.' The city remains a major energy hub with companies like ONEOK, WPX Energy, and numerous oil and gas firms. Tulsa also has a growing aerospace, manufacturing, and healthcare sector. The city's Gathering Place park and Tulsa Arts District have contributed to urban revival.
